metronome.title: Metronome
guideItems: A metronome is an essential tool for maintaining accurate rhythm while playing. Whether you practice piano, guitar, drums, violin, or any other instrument, it helps you develop a solid sense of timing and play at the tempo indicated on the score. This online metronome runs directly in your browser, so you can use it anytime without installing any app.
How to Use BPM

BPM (Beats Per Minute) indicates the number of beats per minute. Use the slider to set any tempo from 40 to 200 BPM. Slow pieces (ballads, adagio) typically use 60–80 BPM, medium tempos (andante, moderato) use 90–120 BPM, and fast pieces (allegro, presto) use 120–200 BPM. The tempo terms shown on screen (Largo, Adagio, Andante, Moderato, Allegro, Presto) help you understand classical tempo markings at a glance.
Time Signature Settings

4/4 time is the most common, with four beats per measure. 3/4 time is used for waltzes, and 2/4 suits marches. 6/8 is a compound duple meter often found in Irish music and some classical pieces. Odd meters like 5/4 appear in jazz tunes such as Take Five. Use the Customize button to set any time signature from 1 to 16 beats, ideal for varied practice sessions.
Using Tap Tempo

The Tap Tempo button lets you tap the beat to measure BPM. Tap 2–4 times in rhythm with the desired tempo, and the metronome will calculate and set the BPM automatically. This is useful when the score has no tempo marking or when you want to match a recording or streaming track. If you stop tapping for more than 3 seconds, the measurement resets—so tap continuously for the best result.
Practice Tips

Start at a slow tempo (60–80 BPM) and focus on locking in the beat. Rather than rushing to play fast, practice slowly and accurately first, then gradually increase the speed. Align your breathing with the downbeat (first beat) of the metronome, and stay relaxed on the weaker beats to improve your rhythm. Drummers and percussionists can practice groove with the metronome, while vocalists and orchestral players can work on phrasing and breath control.
More Use Cases

Beyond practice, metronomes are widely used in composing, recording, and rehearsals. Use it as a click track in your DAW or during recording sessions, or to align tempo in band rehearsals. Knowing the BPM also helps you understand a song's mood and genre. This tool supports 40–200 BPM, making it suitable for most musical styles and practice situations.

tuner.title: Tuner
guideItems: A tuner is an essential tool for tuning your instrument to the correct pitch. Whether you play guitar, bass, violin, ukulele, mandolin, or wind instruments like flute and clarinet, a tuner helps you achieve accurate intonation. This online tuner analyzes sound in real time through your microphone and displays the pitch relative to standard A440. No app installation is required—use it directly in your browser at home, in the practice room, or at the venue.
Standard Pitch A440

A440 refers to the standard frequency of 440 Hz for the note A. It is the international reference (ISO 16) used by most orchestras and instrument makers. Some Baroque or classical ensembles use historical pitches like A415 or A432, but for general practice and performance, A440 is the norm. This tuner calculates pitches based on A440.
Understanding Cents

Cents are 1/100th of a semitone and indicate how far a note is from the correct pitch. A value near 0¢ means the note is in tune; +50¢ means a half step sharp, and -50¢ means a half step flat. The gauge needle moves from left (flat) to right (sharp), and when it points to the center, the note is correctly tuned. Within ±5¢ the display shows 'In Tune,' within ±15¢ it shows 'Close,' and beyond that 'Out of Tune.'
How to Use

Press 'Start Tuner' and allow microphone access when prompted. Then play one string or note at a time—the tuner will display the frequency and note name in real time. Playing multiple strings or notes at once makes detection difficult, so always play one at a time. A quiet environment gives the most accurate results; if there is background noise, bring the microphone closer to your instrument.
Tuning Tips by Instrument

Guitar & Bass: Tune from low to high strings (6th to 1st). After tuning one string, check the previous strings again as they may have shifted. Ukulele: standard tuning is G-C-E-A. Violin, viola, violoncello: fine-tuning matters—wait for the pitch to stabilize before adjusting the pegs. Wind instruments: sustain a single note so the tuner can lock onto the pitch.
Additional Tips

Checking tuning before practice helps you get more out of your sessions. Temperature and humidity can affect pitch, so it's a good idea to verify tuning at the start and midway through practice. This tuner detects frequencies from 70 to 1200 Hz, covering most common instrument ranges. Using headphones or earbuds lets you hear your instrument without the tuner's feedback interfering.

officialAudio.title: Official Audio
officialAudio.subtitle: Convert album art + multiple audio files to MP4 at once.
guideItems: Parallel count auto-adjusts by device performance.
Browser may force-close tab if memory is low.
MP4 filename uses audio filename.
contentGuideItems: Official Audio Generator combines album art images with audio files to create MP4 videos ready for YouTube upload. You can produce 'official audio' style videos for music distribution, streaming uploads, and social sharing without separate video editing software—all in your browser. Multiple audio files can be processed in parallel to save time.

You can select multiple audio files (MP3, WAV, etc.), and one album art image (PNG, JPG) is required. Use a square or 16:9 image at least 1080px wide for best results. The image is displayed as a static frame during playback, and the output uses H.264 video and AAC audio codecs for broad platform compatibility.

Fast (2fps, 720p) encodes fastest and produces smaller files. Balanced (12fps, 1080p) offers a good balance of quality and speed for most official audio. High quality (24fps, 1080p) gives smoother video suitable for music videos or high-quality uploads. Processing time depends on your device and file count; parallel jobs are automatically adjusted based on CPU cores.

Click 'Generate all' to create an MP4 for each audio file. Progress bars show the status of each job, and completed files appear with preview and download links. MP4 filenames match the original audio filenames. If memory runs low, the browser tab may close, so processing fewer files at a time is safer when working with many tracks.

Artists and labels can quickly create official audio videos for Spotify, YouTube Music, Apple Music, and more. Use it for pre-release singles, EPs, compilations, and full albums. No video editing experience is needed—just prepare your image and audio. This is especially useful for indie artists and small labels.

mastering.title: Mastering
mastering.subtitle: Apply simple high-pass/comp/loudness/limiter chain.
guideItems: Presets differ in loudness/limiter settings.
Large files may fail due to browser memory limits.
Speed depends on device and file length.
contentGuideItems: Mastering is the final polish applied to mixed audio. This tool runs a chain of high-pass filter, compressor, loudness normalizer, and limiter to produce levels suitable for streaming or podcast platforms. It runs in the browser using ffmpeg.wasm, so no software installation is required, and multiple files can be processed in parallel.

Streaming (-14 LUFS) matches the recommended loudness for Spotify, Apple Music, YouTube, and most streaming services. Loud (-9 LUFS) suits genres that need more volume, such as EDM, hip-hop, and rock. Podcast (-16 LUFS) is tailored for podcast and voice content. Studio (WAV) outputs a lossless file for further editing or delivery to a mastering engineer. Each preset differs in loudness target and limiter settings.
WAV (pcm_s16le) is lossless and ideal for archiving or further editing. M4A (AAC) is smaller and convenient for streaming uploads. Use M4A for platform uploads and WAV when you need a master archive.

Very long files (e.g., over an hour) or large batches may fail due to browser memory limits. Processing speed depends on CPU and file length. Keep the tab open until jobs complete. The cancel button stops in-progress jobs, but completed files remain available for download.

Ensure your mix is finished before mastering. Heavy clipping or noise is hard to fix with mastering alone. Try different presets to find the right fit, and consider exporting both streaming and loud versions. For complex projects, consider working with a professional mastering engineer.

section1.title: Overview
section1.content: MUSE CORE ('we', 'our', or 'the service') provides music utilities including metronome, tuner, official audio generation, and mastering. This Privacy Policy explains how we handle information when you use our service. We do not require user registration, and we do not collect personal information.

section2.title: Information We Do Not Collect
section2.content: We do not collect personal information such as names, email addresses, phone numbers, or account credentials. There is no sign-up or login process. All processing—including metronome, tuner, audio/video encoding, and mastering—occurs locally in your browser. Your audio files, images, and any other data you use with our tools never leave your device and are not transmitted to our servers or any third party.

section3.title: Browser and Technical Data
section3.content: When you visit our website, your browser may automatically send standard technical information (e.g., IP address, browser type, device type) to enable the site to load and function. We do not use this information to identify or track you personally. We do not use cookies for tracking or advertising purposes. Any local storage (e.g., preferences) remains on your device only.

section4.title: Third-Party Services
section4.content: Our service may use third-party services (e.g., hosting, analytics) to operate the website. These services may collect limited technical data as described in their own privacy policies. We do not share your personal information with third parties because we do not collect it.

section5.title: Changes and Contact
section5.content: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. Continued use of the service after changes constitutes acceptance. If you have questions about this policy, please contact us through the contact page.

section1.title: Acceptance of Terms
section1.content: By accessing or using MUSE CORE ('the service'), you agree to these Terms of Service. If you do not agree, please do not use the service.

section2.title: Free Use and Commercial Use
section2.content: MUSE CORE is provided free of charge. You may use the service for personal, educational, and commercial purposes without payment or attribution. This includes using the metronome, tuner, official audio generator, and mastering tools for your own projects, client work, or commercial releases.

section3.title: Your Content and Responsibility
section3.content: You are solely responsible for the content you upload and process using our tools. You must have the necessary rights to any audio, images, or other materials you use. We do not claim ownership of your content. All processing happens in your browser; we do not store or access your files.

section4.title: Disclaimer of Warranties
section4.content: The service is provided 'as is' and 'as available' without warranties of any kind, express or implied. We do not guarantee that the service will be uninterrupted, error-free, or suitable for any particular purpose. Use of the service is at your own risk.

section5.title: Limitation of Liability
section5.content: To the fullest extent permitted by law, MUSE CORE and its operators shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages arising from your use of the service. We are not responsible for any loss of data, revenue, or business opportunities.

section6.title: Changes and Termination
section6.content: We may modify or discontinue the service at any time. We may also update these Terms of Service. Continued use after changes constitutes acceptance. If you do not agree to updated terms, please stop using the service.
